name: Braggin' Rights Chicken Fried Steak
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Roundsteak, sliced 1/2 inch thick and twice-tenderized by the butcher
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------CLASSIC CREAM GRAVY----------------------------
1/4 c Pan drippings
3 tb All-purpose flour
2 c Evaporated milk
1 c Unsalted beef stock
1/2 tsp Fresh ground black pepper
Salt to taste
----------------------------------OPTIONAL----------------------------------
Mashed potatoes
Homemade buttermilk biscuits
Cut steak into 4 equal portions. Pound until each is about 1/4 inch thick.
Place flour in a shallow bowl. In a second dish, stir together baking powder,
baking soda, black pepper, and salt; mix in buttermilk, egg, hot red pepper
sauce, and minced garlic. The mixture will be thin.
Dredge each steak first in flour, then in batter. Dunk steaks back into
flour, dredging well, patting the surface of the meat until dry.
Add enough Crisco to a deep cast-iron skillet or Dutch oven to deep fry
steaks at least 4 inches deep. Heat shortening to 325 degrees. Fry steaks,
pushing them under fat or turning as they float, for 7 to 8 minutes, or until
golden brown. Drain on paper towels and transfer to a platter. Keep warm while
preparing Classic Cream Gravy.
Divide steaks among plates and serve with mashed potatoes and gravy.
CLASSIC CREAM GRAVY:
After cooking chicken-fried steak or similar dish, pour off the top fat through
a strainer, leaving about 1/2 cup pan drippings in the skillet. Return any
browned cracklings from the strainer to the skillet.
Place skillet over medium heat. Sprinkle flour, stirring to avoid lumps.
Add evaporated milk and beef stock. Simmer until thickened and raw flour taste
is gone, about 3 minutes. Stir gravy frequently, scraping up browned bits.
Season with pepper and salt. Makes about 3 cups.
